title: messages: deserialize_file_name allows single-dot filename
description: deserialize_file_name() in messages.rs rejects path separators and '..' but permits '.' (single dot, which refers to the current directory on POSIX and Windows). A peer could send FileHeader with name: '.' without triggering a protocol error. The receiver's file-write code would attempt to open '.' in the download directory, which would fail at the OS level (cannot open a directory as a file), but the rejection should happen at the protocol layer. Add a check: if s == "." { return Err(...) } alongside the existing '..' check.
